epics.next_action: Legacy epic — shipped pre-spec-template convention. See spec.md stub + stories.json.
Closed reopen-remediation in f53eacf (AC-039 fix + AC-047 spec amendment). AC-045 scenario 5 / B3 retry-attempts flake tracked as separate finding.
Architectural refactor — run /base:arch-debate first; do not /base:feature directly until the receive-engine boundary is debated.

findings.text: make test-e2e-groups was not executed during S7 (Docker/static-build unavailable in worker); the two-client leave flow (AC-MEMBERS-2) passed only structural validation (tsc clean, playwright --list enumerates 9 tests) and has never been run end-to-end in CI.

Makefile/.envrc do not pin PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH; e2e runs depend on a shared /opt cache that drifts across project Playwright versions (see bug-reports/e2e-iteration-2026-05-08.md § C1 for three documented permanent-fix candidates).

DM rendering produced multiple bubbles per single send (3 distinct msg-<rumorId> ids); locator narrowed via .first() in 3ea1716 masks the underlying duplication. The af27581 fix may be incomplete or a different surface. Capture kind:1059 WS frames on Bob to separate publish-vs-subscription-vs-render duplication.

Profile-request memo.attempts stays at 0 under Playwright fake-clock cycling — AC-045 scenario 5 deferred (test.fixme). Real cause undiagnosed; candidates: fake-clock + setInterval interaction, recordEmitted Date.now() racing real-time write, or requestedOnEntryForRef cycling under cross-navigation re-mount. Boundary contract already vitest-covered.

app/src/lib/marmot/chatPersistence.ts (317 LOC) is the largest marmot persistence module — performs IDB writes for group chat history — and has no direct unit test; regressions here only surface via e2e.

app/src/lib/marmot/registerHandlers.ts wires every marmot handler; a regression that silently drops a registration would be invisible until the relevant e2e fails. No unit-level guard on the registration chain.
app/src/lib/keyPackages.ts handles MLS key-package generation/parse (protocol-critical) — no unit test exercises malformed inputs, encoding boundaries, or roundtrip identity.

app/package.json lacks @vitest/coverage-v8 or equivalent; make test-unit runs 866 tests but cannot report line/branch coverage, leaving 'mocks-the-helper-at-the-SUT-call-site' blind spots structurally invisible.

Six of seven React contexts (ChatStore, NostrIdentity, Profile, PollStore, Backup, Marmot) ship substantial state logic with no unit-level guard; only LanguageContext is exercised indirectly via i18n tests.

Seven of nine custom hooks (useDirectReactions, useMoodTheme, useOnlineStatus, useSelectedTopics, useStudyTimer, useThemeStyles, useTopicProgress) have no unit test; only useDecryptedImage and useImageSend are covered.

base:e2e-testing skill runs in a forked context but returns only 'Skill execution completed' to the parent — no pass/fail counts, no failure messages. Caller has to re-run the runner directly to learn whether tests passed.

High: backup/restore is materially lossy while presenting itself as a full-state restore. relayBackup.ts only backs up a small subset of localStorage plus per-group data, and truncates each group chat to MAX_CHAT_MESSAGES = 10 (relayBackup.ts:110, :178). On restore, the code wipes all storage keys including contacts/contact cache (types/index.ts:127) and deletes every quizzl:messages:* thread (relayBackup.ts:364, chatPersistence.ts:306). Result: a restore silently drops DM history, stored contacts/contact metadata, and all but the last 10 group messages.

Medium: unread-count initialization can clobber live increments during startup. unreadStore.ts:125 and :155 recompute state asynchronously then replace counts/joinRequests wholesale, so any message or join request arriving while IDB is being scanned can be lost. The DM path tries to preserve concurrent updates, but :298 still lets stale computed values overwrite newer live counts for peers it re-evaluated, contradicting the comment and lowering the badge count after startup.

Medium: failed backup restore is reported as success. In the error path of settings.tsx:219, a thrown restoreFromBackup() closes the dialog, clears the pending payload, and calls setRestoreSuccess(true) at :229 — producing a false-positive success state precisely when restore has failed.

groups+dm e2e suite scale-bleed: 177-test single-run cumulative had a 1-test failure at #130 (groups-profile-request.spec.ts:149 scenario 1 'aged-history backfill AC-045'), but the same scenario passes in 2.6s when run in isolation. After ~10min the dev server's HMR cache, strfry's tmpfs event log, and per-context IDB state accumulate enough drift to make subscription-driven assertions miss their deadline. Generalises the scenario-5 finding (profile-request-memo-attempts-stays-at) — same root pattern, different AC-045 scenario surfacing it.

Make: no umbrella target runs the full e2e suite. 'make test' runs unit + test-e2e (groups mode only). The fast non-relay suite (86 tests across story-01..08, profile, avatar, banner, emoji, notification-bell) is only reachable via 'make test-e2e-fast' and is not invoked by any aggregate target. Result: a regression in story-* paths can ship without any local Make target catching it. Either fold test-e2e-fast into 'test' or introduce a 'test-e2e-all' that runs both modes back-to-back.

Process gap: the dm-*.spec.ts files added in e9762ec (dm-giftwrap-bell, dm-historical-recovery, dm-self-heal, dm-third-party-inbound) shipped with multiple latent infra bugs and were never executed end-to-end — they were structurally invisible in BOTH run modes because playwright.config testMatch was 'groups-*.spec.ts' for groups mode and testIgnore: 'groups-*.spec.ts' for fast mode, so dm-* matched neither pattern. Bugs found: (a) wrong IDB schema in dm-self-heal (wrote to 'quizzl-messages' DB; app reads 'keyval-store'), (b) reference to global.__testPage never wired up in dm-historical-recovery, (c) removed nostr-tools API getSignature in dm-third-party-inbound, (d) closure-shadowing bug in lp_contacts_v1 seed shared by all four files, (e) stale selectors that no longer match the DOM. All fixed in this session (testMatch widened to ['groups-*', 'dm-*']), but the underlying CI gap remains: structural validation passed for tests that could not run.

ContactChat.tsx uses the same fetch-then-live-sub anti-pattern fixed in welcomeSubscription.ts: Phase-1 runs three parallel fetchEventsWithTimeout calls for DM history then Phase-2 opens three live ndk.subscribe calls, leaving a gap where events are silently lost.

subscribeToGroupMessages creates a fresh processedIds Set on every call; if two subscription instances overlap during rapid re-subscribe (e.g. on groups state change), the same event ID can be dispatched twice across instances — cross-instance dedup requires module-level state or guaranteed teardown ordering.

DM e2e specs (dm-giftwrap-bell, dm-historical-recovery, dm-third-party-inbound) publish gift-wraps / kind-4 events directly to strfry via raw WebSocket from the test process. Violates the publish-via-app guideline (see CLAUDE.md "E2E tests"): tests pass even when the app's NDK/publish path is broken. Rewrite to publish through a second browser.newContext() that boots the peer and calls the app's publish helper. Open question for dm-third-party-inbound: the app likely has no kind-4 NIP-04 publish path; either confirm an existing UI/bridge route, or document this spec as the narrow exception with a fixture-loader pattern rather than inline WebSocket.

The __quizzlPublishDm test bridge lives in unreadStore.ts but has nothing to do with unread counts (module cohesion smell, flagged in DM walled-garden Opus review). Relocating it out of scope for this epic (risk of regressions). Revisit in a dedicated cleanup pass.

ContactChat uses setInterval with 50ms polling for up to 5s to wait for MarmotContext readiness before applying the walled-garden gate on historical events. A cleaner approach would be a whenReady(): Promise<void> method on MarmotContext that resolves when groups are hydrated. Deferred from DM walled-garden epic per reviewer recommendation (out of scope).

archive.text: onMembersChanged fires only when currentMembers.length differs from stored.memberPubkeys.length; a future ghost-cleanup pass that removes a ghost but adds a real member in the same commit would leave the departed member visible in the UI even after AC-MEMBERS-1 passes.
